comment: Added missing author to source and added access date.
/* Iran's participation */ The concerns section needs to be cleaned up somehow, it's so long and hard to follow
/* Prize money */ The Preparation money has been increased by FIFA in 76th Congress.
Removed outdated sources, added missing author to current source, and cleanup.
Reverted 1 edit by [[Special:Contributions/Yaavarr|Yaavarr]] ([[User talk:Yaavarr|talk]]) to last revision by Assadzadeh
/* Ticket prices */ "they" is ambiguous (and misleading when the apparent antecedent is singular).
/* top */ move to note as is the common style in many articles
/* Venues */ no need to pipe, we should be using the
dummy edit to add to last edit summary: we should be using the [[WP:COMMONNAME]] of the stadium reflected in the article title, so piping is unnecessary
/* Opening ceremony */ [[MOS:DATECOMMA]] and elimination of redundancy.
Removing [[:Category:Canada–Mexico sports relations]] per [[Wikipedia:Categories for discussion/Log/2026 March 21#Category:Bilateral cricket relations of Canada]]
Removing [[:Category:Canada–United States sports relations]] per [[Wikipedia:Categories for discussion/Log/2026 March 21#Category:Bilateral cricket relations of Canada]]
/* FIFA Peace Prize */ past tense for singular events
/* Protests and massacres in Iran */ Source misrepresented in that [[Sardar Pashaei]] called for a ban from IOC competitions, not the FIFA World Cup.
/* Team base camps */ Added Morocco source. Needs citations for cape verde and iraq
Moving to the end of the section – part 1
Moving to the end of the section – part 2
/* Team base camps */ Explanation of different names for some training venues
X is not a reliable source
/* Team base camps */ Removed Iraq and Cape Verde camps until source is provided
/* Qualification */ grammar (incorrect uses of the plural form, as further evidenced by the disconnect that edit created in "was the only former champions").
Tournament has not occurred yet, so it doesn't need to be in the past tense. Also, national teams are singular in American English.
/* Venues */ use sort value instead of calling a template due to post-expand include size issues

remove templates per [[WP:BIDIRECTIONAL]]; and [[Template:2026 FIFA World Cup third-place table]] has a large impact on the post-expand include size, so linking to its section on the knockout sub-article instead
/* Final match halftime show */ adding info
/* Final match halftime show */ missing comma.
/* Concerns */ Added link to list article
/* Controversies */ Eliminating items that aren't as prevalent and can be on the list article only
/* Draw */ the draw result is better suited for the [[2026 FIFA World Cup seeding]] sub-article; for this page, readers can see below which teams ended up in which groups
add prose to format in group/knockout stage sections
/* Music */ remove redlinks, unclear if independently notable
/* Posters */ the list of artists for each host city isn't particularly notable; use more encyclopedic voice
moved ticket price criticism to controversies article
/* */ Added an internal website courtesy and reference Linklint to the 3 nations from Wikipedia.

/* Iran's participation */ I feel this section is still too long and should be mostly moved to the new controversies page
Undid revision [[Special:Diff/1354415896|1354415896]] by [[Special:Contributions/Saleah253|Saleah253]] ([[User talk:Saleah253|talk]]) the squads section is already immediately above this...

/* Immigration */ Added a note about the U.S. waiving the visa bond requirement for fans from five African countries, as well as the travel ban on fans from Iran and Haiti, under the Immigration controversies section.
/* Awards */ removing section on "FIFA Peace Prize"; this isn't really related to the 2026 world cup and this page is already quite long, before the tournament has even begun

Undid revision [[Special:Diff/1354643590|1354643590]] by [[Special:Contributions/Amakuru|Amakuru]] ([[User talk:Amakuru|talk]]) undoing removal of a relatively controversial section; this removal should have come only after discussion

Undid revision [[Special:Diff/1354648720|1354648720]] by [[Special:Contributions/Jkudlick|Jkudlick]] ([[User talk:Jkudlick|talk]]) - please see my talk page comment, and [[WP:ONUS]]. If you want to include this material on this page, you need to demonstrate that there is a consensus for it. If (and only if) such a consensus develops in the talk page discussion then you can re-add

Undid revision [[Special:Diff/1354651697|1354651697]] by [[Special:Contributions/Amakuru|Amakuru]] ([[User talk:Amakuru|talk]]) - self-reverting for now; it seems we may retain a small amount of material on this topic, so original version should be the starting point for that.....
/* Match ball */ This one is a more professional picture
/* Group stage */ reducing [[WP:PEIS]] by 207 KiB; see [[Talk:2026 FIFA World Cup#Issues with the post-expand include size]]
/* Controversies */ Moved all info to secondary article as everything being added was just put on this page.

a cut down version of the FIFA Peace Prize detail has been copied over to [[2026 FIFA World Cup draw]], per the talk page; as such, I'm reducing the coverage of that here to one sentence among the summary of the draw; no further coverage of it is warranted on this page
/* Draw */ typo (British spelling in article tagged for American English).

Undid revision [[Special:Diff/1354831767|1354831767]] by [[Special:Contributions/Chris1834|Chris1834]] ([[User talk:Chris1834|talk]]) while this section can be shortened, [[Wikipedia:Summary style|summary style]] needs to be used. There should be more detail than one sentence, and some references are required

Undid revision [[Special:Diff/1354715835|1354715835]] by [[Special:Contributions/Jkudlick|Jkudlick]] ([[User talk:Jkudlick|talk]]) this could be an option, but a number of editors on the talk page were in favor of other options first, and only this as a last resort. Better there be a clear consensus before making such a change
/* Qualification */ Mentioned that this will be the first World Cup since 2010 to not feature Costa Rica
updates on iran and halftime final

/* Final match halftime show */ The first sentence of this section arguably violated [[WP:NPOV]]. "Akin to what happened in" comes across as saying something bad happened or something went wrong. Certainly some people object to the halftime show, but the second paragraph addresses that issue.
/* Music */ grammar and failure to use commas properly.
/* Music */ further grammar corrections. A song is an inanimate thing. It does not release itself.
